ingredients.name: Former Yoshii Shuzo Brick Warehouse
ingredients.address: Former Yoshii Shuzō Brick Warehouse, ２-1 Yoshinocho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8188, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This historic red-brick warehouse in Hirosaki appears in episode 12 as an atmospheric backdrop when Makoto and Chinatsu explore the town together. Its Meiji-era industrial architecture gives the scene a timeless, quietly magical feeling that suits the anime's gentle tone.

ingredients.name: Shimaya
ingredients.entityContext: This small Hirosaki shop appears briefly in episode 12 as part of the everyday town scenery Makoto passes through on her errands, capturing the slice-of-life charm of the series.

ingredients.name: Hirosaki Station
ingredients.address: Hirosaki Station, 1 Omotemachi, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8096, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hirosaki Station is where Makoto Kowata arrives in episode 1, broom and black cat in tow, as she moves to Aomori to train as a witch. It is the very first real-world location fans see in the series.

ingredients.name: Shrine Entrance (Bus)
ingredients.address: Arabia Shriners, 10510 Harwin Dr., Houston, TX 77036

ingredients.name: Taisho Roman Tea Room
ingredients.address: Taishō Roman Tea Room, 藤田記念庭園洋館 1F 8-1 Kamishiroganecho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8207, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Western-style hall of Fujita Kinen Teien modeled in the anime's opening visuals evokes the romantic Taisho-era atmosphere that defines Hirosaki's aesthetic throughout Flying Witch.

ingredients.name: Restaurant YAMANI
ingredients.entityContext: This neighborhood restaurant near the Aoyagi district appears in the opening sequence as part of the rural Hirosaki streetscape that frames Makoto's new everyday life.

ingredients.name: Yamashita Park
ingredients.address: 279 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0023, Japan

ingredients.name: Shimoguchi(Bus)

ingredients.entityContext: Iwakiyama Shrine at the foot of Mt. Iwaki appears in episode 2 when Makoto and Chinatsu visit to pay respects, the towering cedar-lined approach conveying the spiritual atmosphere so central to the anime.

ingredients.name: Hirosaki Castle Botanical Garden
ingredients.address: Hirosaki Castle Botanical Garden, 1 Shimoshiroganecho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8356, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The botanical garden within Hirosaki Castle Park is shown in episode 2 during Makoto and Chinatsu's excursion, its lush greenery and quiet paths embodying the series' slow-life countryside mood.

ingredients.name: Saishoin Jido Park
ingredients.address: Saishoin Jido Park, 80 Doyamachi, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8196, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small park near Saishoin temple appears in episode 2 as Makoto and Chinatsu walk through Hirosaki's older neighborhood streets, part of the everyday pilgrimage route fans recreate.

ingredients.name: Aoyagi(Bus)
ingredients.address: Aoyagi, Aoyagimachi, Fujikawa, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 400-0501, Japan

ingredients.name: Y.Y.Gold Farm Processing Plant
ingredients.entityContext: This apple farm processing facility near Hirosaki appears in episode 2 as part of the agricultural countryside scenery, reflecting Aomori's famous apple orchards that give the anime its distinctive rural character.

ingredients.name: Shinmeigu
ingredients.address: Asagaya Shinmeigū, 1 Chome-25-5 Asagayakita, Suginami City, Tokyo 166-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Hirosaki Castle Outer Moat
ingredients.address: Hirosaki Castle Outer Moat, Shimoshiroganecho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8356, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The outer moat of Hirosaki Castle appears in episode 4 as Makoto strolls along the water's edge, the castle district's historic atmosphere serving as a backdrop for her quiet witchcraft studies.

ingredients.name: Hirosaki Castle Sannomaru Ōtemon Gate
ingredients.address: Hirosaki Castle Sannomaru Ōtemon Gate, １ Shimoshiroganecho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8356, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Otemon Gate of Hirosaki Castle's third enclosure features in episode 4 when Makoto passes through the castle grounds, the handsome 17th-century gate one of the most recognizable landmarks in the series.

ingredients.name: Gejo-bashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Gejō-bashi Bridge, １ Shimoshiroganecho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8356, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Gejo-bashi Bridge leads to Hirosaki Castle's keep and appears in episode 4 as Makoto crosses toward the castle, a classically scenic shot replicated by virtually every Flying Witch pilgrim.

ingredients.name: Yoshita-bashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Ebisu Bridge, 1 Chome-6 Dotonbori, Chuo Ward, Osaka, 542-0071, Japan

ingredients.name: Hane-bashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Hane-bashi Bridge, １ Shimoshiroganecho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8356, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hane-bashi Bridge within Hirosaki Castle Park appears in episode 4 alongside the moat sequence, its graceful stone arch framing views of the park that fans photograph in recreation of the episode.

ingredients.name: Message Art Gallery Kanku no Ie
ingredients.localName: メッセージアートギャラリー観空の宇
ingredients.entityContext: This small Aoyagi-district gallery building appears in episode 5 as background scenery in the rural Hirosaki neighborhood where Makoto and friends walk, adding local color to the anime's countryside setting.

ingredients.name: Chiin no Sato Aoyagi
ingredients.entityContext: This facility in the Aoyagi neighborhood is shown in episode 5 as part of the quiet residential streets Makoto passes through, capturing the unhurried rural life the anime celebrates.

ingredients.address: Japan, 〒315-0001 Ibaraki, Ishioka, 1 Chome−８−5 SKILL石岡

ingredients.entityContext: This small Aoyagi-area shop appears in episode 5 as a background building in the slice-of-life streetscape Makoto navigates, part of the detailed real-world Hirosaki environment the anime recreates.

ingredients.address: Kamisukisawa, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-1504, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kamisukisawa area in the Hirosaki outskirts appears in episode 5 as a rural mountain road setting, the wooded landscape evoking the sense of wilderness just beyond Makoto's new hometown.

ingredients.address: Lawson Hirosaki Yuguchi Store, Ichinohosokawa-51-3 Yuguchi, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-1501, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This convenience store appears in episode 6 as Makoto stops by for everyday errands, one of the series' characteristically mundane real-world locations that grounds the magical story in ordinary life.

ingredients.name: Tochinai Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: Tochinai Bridge in the Aoyagi district appears in episode 6 as a neighborhood crossing Makoto uses, part of the dense web of real Hirosaki locations that fans map out on pilgrimage routes.

ingredients.entityContext: This riverside open ground appears in episode 11 as an outdoor space where characters gather, the flat Tsuchibuchi River floodplain setting capturing Hirosaki's expansive rural feel.

ingredients.name: Hirosaki Municipal Tourist Information Center
ingredients.address: Hirosaki Municipal Tourist Center, 追手門広場 内 ２-1 Shimoshiroganecho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8356, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The tourist information center next to the Otemon Gate appears in episode 12 as a backdrop in the castle district, frequently pictured by pilgrims who follow Makoto's final episode route through Hirosaki.

ingredients.name: Tsuchibuchi River Yoshinocho Green Space
ingredients.address: Tsuchibuchi River Yoshinochō Green Space, ２-7 Yoshinocho, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8188, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This riverside green space along the Tsuchibuchi River appears in episode 12 near the Yoshii warehouse district, its cherry-tree-lined path a quietly beautiful final-episode location for fans to walk.

ingredients.name: Narita Iron Works
ingredients.entityContext: This small ironworks building appears in episode 1 as Makoto first walks through the rural outskirts of Hirosaki after arriving, establishing the working-class countryside atmosphere of the anime.

ingredients.name: AEON TOWN Hirosaki Hinokuchi

ingredients.address: AEON TOWN Hirosaki Hinokuchi, 2 Chome-９-6 Hinokuchi, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8278, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The AEON TOWN shopping center near Hinokuchi appears in episode 1 as Makoto's relatives drive past on the way home from the station, a grounding modern landmark in the otherwise traditional-feeling setting.

ingredients.name: DCM Hirosaki Hinokuchi Store
ingredients.address: DCM Hirosaki Hinokuchi Store, 2 Chome-８-8 Hinokuchi, Hirosaki, Aomori 036-8278, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This home improvement store adjacent to AEON TOWN appears in episode 1 as part of the same suburban commercial strip Makoto passes on her first ride through Hirosaki.

ingredients.name: Ishibaya Liquor Store
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional liquor shop in Hirosaki's Miharashi district appears in episode 1 as background scenery in the older part of town, contributing to the anime's rich sense of Hirosaki's local character.

ingredients.name: Tsukuta Architecture
ingredients.entityContext: This local architectural firm's building appears in episode 1 as part of the suburban Hirosaki streetscape Makoto encounters on her first journey through town, one of the many real-world details mapped by pilgrims.
